wait_for.note: journey_updates (the seating UPDATE's Aurora write) FKs to trip_details, not passenger -- see seed/feeds/prelude.py's module docstring caveat: AuroraSource has no dedicated trip_details probe today, so wait_for(..., 'trip_details', ...) proxies through trip().

prelude.note: seed/feeds/prelude.py's build_create_payload(payload, revert_fields) consumes revert_fields verbatim (same dotted/[n] path syntax as seed/engine.set_dotpath). This base template's own seating.seats[0].number (14C) already IS the CREATE-prelude's pre-change default (contrail's SCS-01 baseline) -- a per-case render (not yet wired) would set the manifest's mutable seat to the case's real target (the UPDATE's to-seat), and build_create_payload would revert products[0].seating.seats[0].number back to 14C for the synthetic CREATE burst only, so the later real UPDATE (a genuine 14C->new-seat change) lands. See README.md.

note: SeatChange seed base: pnr + ticket ONLY (no FDM/disruption leg, no EMD, no DDS pin) -- an UPDATE feed's CREATE-prelude parent. The products[0].seating block (seats[0].number=14C) is the pre-existing seat assignment the CREATE-prelude establishes before the real seat-change UPDATE fires. See README.md and docs/superpowers/plans/2026-07-17-nc-seatchange-seed.md.
